latest.title: Rock layers store ancient environments in stripes
latest.script_key: rock layers are time stamps made of stone watch the stripe pattern because each layer can mark a different setting river mud ocean floor windblown sand or volcanic ash stack enough layers and the cliff becomes a timeline which rock clue should we read next

latest.title: Bees don’t just pollinate—they taste with their feet
latest.script_key: bees taste with their feet watch this one twice those little tarsus pads they re tiny taste buds that s why they tap flowers before drinking that s how they know if the nectar s worth it because if it s bad they skip ahead would you trust a mouthful from a flower this finicky

latest.title: Black butterflies taste flowers with their feet
latest.script_key: this black beauty tastes with its feet that s why it lands then walks watch its feet tap the flower not its mouth butterflies have taste sensors on their feet not tongues so it tastes the whole garden in two steps did you learn this wrong too

latest.title: Bees plant pollen like tiny farmers
latest.script_key: watch the back legs see those yellow lumps those aren t just dust bees pack pollen in their pollen baskets like grocery bags they brush it off flowers with their front legs and shove it in that s why they visit hundreds of blooms in one trip they re not just eating they re building stores

latest.title: Owls see your desk light from a mile away
latest.script_key: your desk light screams that s why the owl outside froze mid hover owls see infrared no they see the heat your screen leaks as bright as a traffic light that s why mice and your snack drawer aren t safe tonight did you leave a lamp on last night

latest.title: Horses point their ears toward what matters

latest.title: Goats see wide with rectangular pupils

latest.title: Cats groom to reset their scent map
